SCAM_AWARENESS.TITLE: Scam Awareness
SCAM_AWARENESS.SUBTITLE: Know the common tactics scammers use in India and how to stay safe.
SCAM_AWARENESS.QUIZ_SUBTITLE: Can you spot the fraud in these real-life scenarios?

SCAMS.TITLE: OTP Scam
SCAMS.HOW: Scammer calls impersonating a bank official, citing 'account verification' or 'card block', and asks for the OTP sent to your phone.
RED_FLAGS: Call from a normal mobile number
SCAMS.SCRIPT: Sir, I am calling from SBI head office. Your credit card will be blocked in 10 minutes. I have sent a verification code to your phone, please tell it to me to keep it active.
SCAMS.TODO: Disconnect immediately. No bank asks for OTP.

SCAMS.TITLE: KYC Update Scam
SCAMS.HOW: SMS or call claiming your Paytm/Bank KYC has expired and you need to click a link or download an app (like AnyDesk) to update.
SCAMS.SCRIPT: Dear customer, your PAYTM KYC has expired. Call 98xxxxxxx to update or your wallet will be closed today.
SCAMS.TODO: Go to the official app or branch. Never use phone numbers or links from SMS.

SCAMS.TITLE: UPI Collect Request
SCAMS.HOW: Scammer sends a 'Collect Request' on your UPI app (GPay/PhonePe) while claiming they are 'sending' you money for an OLX item.
SCAMS.SCRIPT: I have sent the payment for the sofa. Just click 'Accept' and enter your PIN to receive it in your bank.
SCAMS.TODO: Remember: YOU NEVER NEED TO ENTER PIN TO RECEIVE MONEY.

SCAMS.TITLE: Fake Job Scam
SCAMS.HOW: Offers of high-paying remote jobs from 'Amazon' or 'Instagram'. They ask for a 'registration fee' or 'laptop security deposit'.
RED_FLAGS: Paying money to get a job
Too good to be true salary
SCAMS.SCRIPT: Work from home 2 hours daily and earn ₹5000/day. Just pay ₹500 for the training kit to start.
SCAMS.TODO: Real companies never ask for money to hire you.

SCAMS.TITLE: FedEx/Police Scam
SCAMS.HOW: A call claiming a package in your name was seized with drugs. They connect you to 'Skype' with fake police to demand money for clearance.
RED_FLAGS: Police asking for money over Skype
Claims of illegal items in your name
SCAMS.SCRIPT: Your FedEx parcel to Taiwan contains 5 passports and drugs. You are under digital arrest. Pay ₹2 Lakh as security bond to avoid jail.
SCAMS.TODO: Police never do 'digital arrests' or ask for money online. Report to 1930.

SCENARIOS.TEXT: SMS: Your SBI account has been credited with ₹25,000 cashback. Click here to claim: bit.ly/sbi-rewards-now
SCENARIOS.REASON: Banks don't use bit.ly links for rewards. This is a phishing link.

SCENARIOS.TEXT: WhatsApp: I'm from your HR department. Please share your Aadhaar photo for the new insurance policy.
SCENARIOS.REASON: HR departments often collect documents for insurance, but verify the sender's identity through official channels first.

SCENARIOS.TEXT: Call: This is the electricity board. Your bill is pending. Pay via this link or power will be cut in 1 hour.
SCENARIOS.REASON: Electricity boards never threaten immediate disconnection via phone calls or random links.

SCENARIOS.TEXT: OLX: I want to buy your fridge. I'm sending a QR code. Scan it and enter your PIN to get the money.
SCENARIOS.REASON: Scanning QR and entering PIN is for SENDING money, not receiving.

SCENARIOS.TEXT: Email: Your Netflix subscription failed. Update payment here: netflix.com/your-account
SCENARIOS.REASON: This looks like a standard notification. Always check if the sender email is actually @netflix.com.

IR.TITLE: Incident Response Playbooks
IR.SUBTITLE: Calm, step-by-step recovery guides for cyber emergencies.
IR.COMPLETION_MSG: 🎉 You have completed all recommended steps for this incident.

SENIOR.TITLE: CyCySafeClick Senior Safe Mode
SENIOR.INPUT_DESC: Type or paste what someone told you:
SENIOR.PLACE_HOLDER: Example: Your bank account will be closed. Please share OTP...
SENIOR.SCAM_SUB: Do not share any details. Do not send money.
SENIOR.SAFE_SUB: We did not detect obvious scam words. Still, stay cautious.
SENIOR.COMMON_TITLE: Common Scams to Watch Out For

BANK.TITLE: 1. Fake Bank Employee Calls
BANK.SAY: They will say: "Sir, your account will close, share OTP now"
BANK.DO: What to do: HANG UP. Call your bank directly on the number on your card.

PENSION.TITLE: 2. Pension/PF Scam Calls
PENSION.SAY: They will say: "Your pension has stopped, pay 500 rupees for form processing"
PENSION.DO: What to do: Ignore. Pension offices NEVER ask for payment over phone.

BILL.TITLE: 3. Fake Electricity/Gas Bill Calls
BILL.SAY: They will say: "Your electricity will be cut tonight, pay bill on this link"
BILL.DO: What to do: Ignore. Call your actual electricity provider using an old bill.

GRANDCHILD.TITLE: 4. Grandchild Emergency Scam
GRANDCHILD.SAY: They will say: "I am in an accident, please send money"
GRANDCHILD.DO: What to do: Hang up. Call your relative on their known original number to verify.

MEDICINE.TITLE: 5. Fake Medicine/Health Cures
MEDICINE.SAY: They will do: Sell "miracle" treatments for diabetes or BP on WhatsApp.
MEDICINE.DO: What to do: Do not buy. Only trust prescribed medicine from your doctor.

SENIOR.GUIDE_DESC: A simple one-page guide to print and keep at home.

QUESTIONS.Q: Do you use the same password for multiple accounts (Email, Bank, Social Media)?

OPTIONS.TIP: Use unique passwords for every account. Use a password manager to help.
Avoid reusing passwords even for 'minor' accounts.

QUESTIONS.Q: Do you have Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) enabled on your primary email and banking apps?

OPTIONS.TIP: Enable 2FA immediately. It's the best defense after a password.
Your email is the keys to your digital life. Protect it with 2FA too.

QUESTIONS.Q: Has anyone ever asked for your OTP (over call or SMS) and did you share it?

OPTIONS.TIP: NO official will EVER ask for your OTP. Sharing it is sharing your money.

QUESTIONS.Q: How often do you update your phone's operating system and apps?

OPTIONS.TEXT: Rarely/When I get a new phone
OPTIONS.TIP: Updates contain critical security patches. Enable auto-updates.
Try to update within a week of a new release.

OPTIONS.TEXT: As soon as update is available

QUESTIONS.Q: Do you use public WiFi (Airports, Cafes) for banking or UPI payments?

OPTIONS.TIP: Public WiFi is easily snooped. Use mobile data for sensitive tasks.

OPTIONS.TEXT: Only if I use a VPN

QUESTIONS.Q: Is your phone protected by a PIN, Pattern, or Biometric (Face/Fingerprint)?

OPTIONS.TIP: Set a screen lock immediately. A stolen phone is an open wallet.
Use a strong 6-digit PIN or complex pattern.

QUESTIONS.Q: Do you check 'App Permissions' before installing a new app from Play Store/App Store?

OPTIONS.TIP: Check why a calculator app needs access to your contacts or SMS.

QUESTIONS.Q: Do you have a backup of your important photos and documents?

OPTIONS.TIP: Ransomware can lock your files. Keep a backup on cloud or physical drive.

QUESTIONS.Q: If you receive a 'Reward' link on WhatsApp from an unknown number, do you click it?

OPTIONS.TIP: WhatsApp rewards are 99% scams. Delete and block.

QUESTIONS.Q: Do you use a different UPI PIN than your phone screen lock PIN?

OPTIONS.TIP: Keep your UPI PIN unique. If someone sees your screen PIN, they can't pay.

REPORT_CYBERCRIME.TITLE: Report Cybercrime

STEPS.TITLE: Visit Official Portal
STEPS.DESC: Go to cybercrime.gov.in and click on 'File a Complaint'.

STEPS.TITLE: Select Category
STEPS.DESC: Choose between 'Financial Fraud', 'Women/Child Related Crime', or 'Other Cyber Crimes'.

STEPS.TITLE: Fill Details
STEPS.DESC: Provide your mobile number, OTP, and details of the incident.

STEPS.TITLE: Upload Evidence
STEPS.DESC: Attach screenshots of messages, transaction receipts, and URLs.

STEPS.TITLE: Submit & Save
STEPS.DESC: Submit and note down the Acknowledgement Number for future tracking.

REPORT_CYBERCRIME.CHECKLIST_INTRO: Gather these before you start reporting:
CHECKLIST_ITEMS: Screenshots of the scam message/call log
Transaction ID / UTR Number (for money theft)
Website URL or App name used
Date and exact time of incident
REPORT_CYBERCRIME.FOOTER_SOURCE: Source: cybercrime.gov.in | National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal

BREACH_CHECK.TITLE: Data Breach Check
BREACH_CHECK.SUBTITLE: Password check only. Email lookup is disabled in the public demo.
BREACH_CHECK.PRIVACY_EMAIL: Email breach lookup is disabled for privacy. We do not send email data to any server.
BREACH_CHECK.EMAIL_DISABLED_NOTE: Email checks are unavailable in this public demo.
BREACH_CHECK.PRIVACY_PWD: Uses the Pwned Passwords API with k-anonymity. Your password is hashed locally before checking.
BREACH_CHECK.RESULT_PWD_DANGER: Warning! This password has appeared in {{ count }} data breaches.
BREACH_CHECK.RESULT_PWD_SAFE: Good news! This password was not found in any known data breaches.
BREACH_CHECK.RESULT_EMAIL_DANGER: Email lookup is disabled in this public demo.
BREACH_CHECK.RESULT_EMAIL_SAFE: Email lookup is disabled in this public demo.
ACTIONS: Change your password for this service immediately.
If you use this password elsewhere, change it there too.
Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) on your accounts.
Monitor your bank statements for suspicious activity.

PHISHING_DETECTOR.TITLE: Phishing Detector
PHISHING_DETECTOR.SUBTITLE: Scan suspicious URLs or SMS messages for signs of fraud.
PHISHING_DETECTOR.MSG_PLACEHOLDER: Paste the SMS or WhatsApp message here...

TLD.LABEL: Suspicious Domain Extension
TLD.DESC: Uses a cheap domain extension often favored by scammers.

IP.LABEL: IP Address Used
IP.DESC: Legitimate sites use brand names, not raw IP addresses.

SHORTENER.LABEL: URL Shortener Detected
SHORTENER.DESC: Scammers use these to hide the real destination.

URGENCY.LABEL: Urgency Tactic
URGENCY.DESC: Scammers create panic to make you act without thinking.

PRIZE.LABEL: Prize/Incentive Claim
PRIZE.DESC: Unexpected prizes are almost always scams.

SENSITIVE.LABEL: Sensitive Info Request
SENSITIVE.DESC: No bank or official will ever ask for your OTP or PIN over text.

ADVICE.DANGER:
1. Do NOT click any links in this message.
2. Do NOT share any OTP or personal details.
3. Report this to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.
4. Forward spam SMS to 1909 (TRAI).
ADVICE.SAFE: This message seems okay, but always stay cautious. Never share passwords or OTPs regardless of how legitimate a request looks.

PWD_LAB.TITLE: Password Lab
PWD_LAB.SUBTITLE: Test your password strength and generate safer alternatives.
PWD_LAB.VARIANTS_EMPTY: Enter a password on the left to see variants.

TIPS.YEAR: Avoid using birth years like 1990 or 2005.
TIPS.COMMON_NAME: Avoid common Indian names, cities, or cricket terms.
TIPS.EXCELLENT: Excellent! This is a very strong password.

FAQ.Q: Why length matters more than complexity?
FAQ.A: Adding just one character increases the possible combinations exponentially. A 12-character simple word is often harder to crack than an 8-character complex one.
A passphrase is a sequence of random words (e.g., "mango-blue-bicycle"). They are easy for humans to remember but very hard for computers to guess.
No. OTP is a second layer, but if your password is weak, hackers can still try to trick you into sharing the OTP. Never share your OTP with anyone.

DEEPFAKE.TITLE: Deepfake & AI Voice Clone Awareness Hub
DEEPFAKE.SUBTITLE: Learn to spot and defend against India's fastest-growing cyber threat.

S1.TITLE: 1. What is a Deepfake?
S1.P1: Imagine someone took your photo and put your face on a completely different body in a video. AI can now do this automatically and realistically in minutes.
S1.P2: Using Deep Learning, scammers can swap faces or perfectly clone anyone's voice using just a 3-second audio clip.

VIDEO.TITLE: 📹 Video Call Impersonation
VIDEO.DESC: A scammer creates a deepfake video of a 'police officer' or 'CBI official' on WhatsApp. They threaten you with fake arrest warrants.

AUDIO.TITLE: 🗣️ Fake Audio Clone Calls
AUDIO.DESC: AI clones your relative's voice. They call saying: 'Beta mujhe ₹50,000 bhejo, main hospital mein hoon'. You think it's actually them.

CEO.TITLE: 👔 Fake CEO/Boss Voice
CEO.DESC: Clones a company CEO's voice to instruct the finance team to do an urgent wire transfer.

CELEB.TITLE: 🌟 Fake Celebrity Endorsements
CELEB.DESC: Deepfake videos of Ratan Tata or Virat Kohli promoting fake trading apps on Facebook.

SEX.TITLE: 📸 Sextortion Deepfakes
SEX.DESC: Scammers take innocent social media photos and use AI to create explicit fake images for blackmail.

S4.TITLE: 4. The Family "Safe Word" System
S4.DESC: Set up a family code word right now that no AI can know.
S4.NOTE: Stored locally. Not sent to any server.
S4.SHARE_TIP: Share this word offline with your family.

S5.TITLE: 5. What to do if you are a victim
STEPS: Report to Platform: Block and flag the account.
StopNCII.org: Block sharing of explicit images.

QUIZ.TITLE: 6. Spot the Deepfake Quiz
QUIZ.REVIEW: Good effort. Review the signs above.

QUIZ.DESC: Test your knowledge, compete nationally, and earn your certificate.

QUIZ.ALREADY_DONE: You have already completed this week's challenge. Come back next Monday!
QUIZ.CERT_SUB: This certifies that scored points in the Weekly Cyber Championship.
QUIZ.ENCOURAGEMENT: Good effort! Read more CyCySafeClick safety tips and try again next week to earn your certificate (Requires 80+ points).

FACT.TITLE: WhatsApp Forward Fact-Checker
FACT.SUBTITLE: Verify viral messages, claims, and links before you share them with others.
FACT.LABEL_MSG: Paste the full WhatsApp forwarded message:
FACT.LABEL_CLAIM: Type the claim you want to verify:
FACT.LABEL_URL: Paste the link/URL that was shared:
FACT.RESOURCES_SUB: Use these trusted portals to search if a viral claim has already been debunked.
GUIDE_STEPS: Google the claim in quotes ("exact claim text").
Check the URL to ensure it's not a mimic site.
Check the date - old news is often recycled.

JOB_SCAM.TITLE: Job Offer Scam Detector
JOB_SCAM.SUBTITLE: Analyze WhatsApp messages, SMS, or job descriptions for hidden scam patterns.
JOB_SCAM.LABEL: Paste the job offer text below:
JOB_SCAM.PLACEHOLDER: Example: Work from home data entry. Earn ₹15,000 daily...
JOB_SCAM.NO_SIGNALS: Not enough clear signals. Always verify independently.
RULES: Verify company on MCA21 portal: mca.gov.in
Check employee reviews on Glassdoor or Ambitionbox
NEVER pay any fees before receiving a salary.

DATA.TITLE: 1. Envelope Stuffing / Data Entry
DATA.DESC: Promise high pay for basic work, but demand a 'registration fee' upfront.

TASK.TITLE: 2. Task-Based App Scams
TASK.DESC: Pay small amounts first, then ask for 'investment' to unlock premium tasks.

BPO.TITLE: 3. Fake BPO / Call Center Jobs
BPO.DESC: Fake agencies charge a 'training fee' for a job at a major IT company.

FOREIGN.TITLE: 4. Foreign Job / Visa Scams
FOREIGN.DESC: Lucrative Dubai/Canada jobs requiring immediate 'visa fees'.

MLM.TITLE: 5. Reselling / MLM Traps
MLM.DESC: Aggressive recruitment to resell overpriced products.

INVESTMENT.TITLE: Crypto & Investment Scam Analyzer
INVESTMENT.SUBTITLE: Protect your hard-earned money from fraudulent schemes and Ponzi traps.

OFFER.TITLE: Analyze an Offer
OFFER.DESC: Paste an app name, Telegram group description, or WhatsApp message.
OFFER.PLACEHOLDER: Example: Join our VIP Telegram group. Invest ₹5000 and get guaranteed 5% daily return...
OFFER.SAFE_SUB: However, always verify if the entity is SEBI registered before investing.

PONZI.TITLE: Ponzi Detection Calculator
PONZI.DESC: Enter the promised returns to see if they are mathematically realistic.

EDU.TITLE: Common Investment Scam Patterns in India

TASK.TITLE: 📱 Task-Based Investment Scam
TASK.DESC: Join Telegram -> Small tasks -> Small pay -> Trust -> Big investment -> Money locked.

PIG.TITLE: 💔 Pig Butchering (Sha Zhu Pan)
PIG.DESC: Romance + investment combo. Scammer builds relationship over months then 'introduces' fake platform.

TRADING.TITLE: 📉 Fake Trading Apps
TRADING.DESC: Legitimate looking app. Small withdrawals work. Big investment gets blocked.

CELEB.TITLE: 🎭 Celebrity-Endorsed Crypto Scams
CELEB.DESC: AI Deepfake videos of famous billionaires promoting fake platforms.

ECOMMERCE.TITLE: e-Commerce Fraud Shield
ECOMMERCE.SUBTITLE: Verify deals, sellers, and learn how to recover your money if scammed online.

FORM.TITLE: Enter Deal Details
FORM.TIMER: Site has a "Limited Time Offer" countdown timer
FORM.PREPAID: Site ONLY accepts UPI/Card (No Cash on Delivery)

REVIEWS.TITLE: How to Spot Fake Reviews
REVIEWS.SUB: Many sellers buy 5-star reviews to trick you. Look for these red flags:

FLAGS.TITLE: Date Clustering:
FLAGS.DESC: Dozens of 5-star reviews posted on the exact same day or week.

FLAGS.TITLE: Generic Praise:
FLAGS.DESC: Reviews say "great product" without mentioning specific features.

FLAGS.TITLE: Hidden Profiles:
FLAGS.DESC: Reviewer has no profile photo, or this is their only review.

FLAGS.TITLE: Missing Photos:
FLAGS.DESC: Real buyers often upload photos. Fake reviews rarely have them.

SELLER_STEPS.TITLE: Seller Verification Steps
LIST: Verify GST: Check the seller's GST number at gst.gov.in.
Check Forums: Search on MouthShut, Trustpilot, or consumer forums.
Google Search: Search for "[Seller Name] + complaints".

RECOVERY.TITLE: I Think I Was Scammed. What Now?
RECOVERY.SUB: Follow this timeline to maximize your chances of getting your money back.
